findings.rule_broken: Pass 4 file-structure / Phase 2 duplication map — flag parallel implementations
findings.evidence: Two metadata-injection engines with divergent header dialects: TS src/comment.ts:25-28 sentinel `>>> l9:meta >>>`/`<<< l9:meta <<<`; Python tools/consolidation/modes/repo_pack/injector.py:11-19 HTML-comment `<!-- L9_META ... /L9_META -->`. Both classify+hash+inject+dedup the same artifact metadata.
findings.impact: A file stamped by the Python tool vs the TS injector gets two incompatible blocks; neither strips the other's sentinels, so re-runs duplicate metadata and idempotency is lost across the toolchain boundary.
findings.correction: Declare one engine authoritative (the shipped TS package) and reduce the other to calling it or explicitly out-of-scope; or define one shared header-dialect contract both read/write/strip.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 1 context lock — as-designed doc must describe the as-built primary artifact
findings.evidence: docs/architecture.md:5-18 documents only a Python layering (ingress.py, core/ scanner->hasher->classifier, modes/<mode>/injector.py), but package.json main=dist/index.js ships the TS pipeline (src/pipeline.ts). No architecture doc describes the src/*.ts engine that is the actual npm package.
findings.impact: The authoritative architecture reference doesn't cover the shipped artifact; conformance can't be checked against a spec and contributors design against the wrong model.
findings.correction: Add/replace architecture.md to document the TS pipeline (retrieval→extract→classify→normalize_meta→inject→verify→compiler) as primary; scope the Python tool as secondary.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 8 leverage/simplicity — silent fallback instead of typed error; silent zero-result on a promised capability
findings.evidence: src/compiler.ts:17 buildDedupReport ignores _threshold/_prefixLen and line 25 hardcodes `nearDuplicates: []`. Config promises near-dup detection (schema.ts:155 nearDupThreshold) and buildDedupEntries computes hashPrefix (compiler.ts:11) never consumed. pipeline.ts:93 passes them through.
findings.impact: Callers configuring nearDupThreshold silently get no near-duplicate detection; the report always reports zero, a fail-open no-op masquerading as a working feature.
findings.correction: Implement prefix/threshold near-duplicate clustering using the computed hashPrefix, or remove nearDupThreshold/hashPrefixLength from public config and report unsupported (fail-closed).

findings.rule_broken: Pass 5 schema/interface alignment — no contract types redefined in multiple places
findings.evidence: FieldDiff declared twice: src/schema.ts:117 (action: 'add'|'revise'|'append-union'|'keep'|'replace') and src/reconcile_fields.ts:16 (action: ReconcileAction). Collision forces alias at boundary: src/index.ts:7 `FieldDiff as ReconcileFieldDiff`.
findings.impact: Two separately-maintained definitions of the same contract type will drift; consumers importing from different modules get subtly different types.
findings.correction: Delete the reconcile_fields.ts copy and import FieldDiff (and ReconcileAction) from schema.ts as the single source of truth.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 4/5 — duplicated logic + config drift between copies
findings.evidence: Two divergent hand-rolled YAML serializers: src/normalize_meta.ts:31 yamlScalar(v) (bare when safe) vs src/inventory.ts:159 `${k}: ${JSON.stringify(v)}` (always double-quoted, lists via JSON.stringify). Two parsers: src/inject.ts:20 parseExistingMeta and src/meta_schema.ts:90 parseCanonicalYaml, each with own coercion.
findings.impact: A header written by inventory (JSON-quoted) round-trips differently than one from the pipeline (bare scalars) when re-read by parseExistingMeta, breaking byte-stable idempotency and risking value corruption.
findings.correction: Consolidate on one YAML serializer and one parser shared by normalize_meta, inventory, inject, and meta_schema.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 9 leverage — overbuilt/dead contract; behavior claimed but absent
findings.evidence: src/schema.ts:177-290 defines the full MetaV3 nine-plane contract (META_V3_PLANES, IdentityPlane..LineagePlane, interface MetaV3 at :280), and docs/contracts.md:94-100 asserts runtime behavior ('carries exactly the nine planes', 'Idempotent: skip if a v3 record present'). No builder/serializer/validator: grep MetaV3|IdentityPlane|META_V3_PLANES src/ outside schema.ts returns nothing.
findings.impact: A documented, versioned contract with idempotency guarantees has zero implementation; consumers may rely on v3 semantics nothing enforces or emits.
findings.correction: Implement the v3 compile/validate/idempotency path (buildMetaV3 + serializer + presence check), or move v3 types and the contracts.md section to 'planned, not implemented'.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 6 schema/field alignment — no duplicated shared contract types
findings.evidence: SharingScope defined identically twice: src/schema.ts:14 and src/namespace.ts:4 (`'private'|'shared'|'agnostic'`). namespace.ts uses its local copy; normalize_meta.ts imports the schema.ts copy.
findings.impact: The scope vocabulary can drift between definitions, silently splitting the contract used by namespace resolution vs header serialization.
findings.correction: Remove the namespace.ts declaration and import SharingScope from schema.ts.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 4 authority-boundary — single source of truth per shared concept
findings.evidence: Four competing 'artifact_type' vocabularies: src/schema.ts:4 ArtifactType (10 values), src/schema.ts:301 SemanticArtifactClass (17), src/inventory.ts:16 InventoryArtifactType (12), and docs/contracts.md:28 taxonomy (matched by tools/consolidation/core/classifier.py:4-11).
findings.impact: 'artifact_type' means four different things by module; cross-tool records can't be joined or validated against one enum, defeating a graphable metadata layer.
findings.correction: Designate one canonical artifact-type enum and express others as typed mappings onto it, or rename each distinctly (coarse_type vs semantic_class vs inventory_type).

findings.evidence: The injector engine doesn't dogfood its own metadata: src/*.ts carry only plain // purpose comments (e.g. pipeline.ts:1) and none contain the injector's own `>>> l9:meta >>>` block or L9_META header. grep -l matches only comment.ts/verify.ts which merely define the sentinels.
findings.impact: The repo can't demonstrate its own metadata contract on itself, weakening confidence the injection/verify path is correct on real trees.
findings.correction: Run the injector over its own src/ as a CI step (or commit stamped headers), turning self-injection into a behavior test.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 4 dangerous-primitive scan (untrusted deserialization / prototype-key sink)
findings.evidence: src/meta_schema.ts:105 `root[key]=SCALAR(rest)`, :161 `map[mm[1]]=SCALAR(mm[2])`, :202 `fields[f.name]=val`; src/inject.ts:37 `result[kv[1]]=val.replace(...)`. loadMetaSchema (src/inventory.ts:77) feeds an operator schema file into these key-assignment sinks with no __proto__/constructor/prototype guard.
findings.impact: Hand-rolled YAML/frontmatter parsers write attacker-influenced keys onto plain objects with no reserved-key filter. Global prototype pollution reachability UNKNOWN (assignments are shallow), but a defense-in-depth gap: a __proto__/constructor key mutates the target's prototype.
findings.correction: Add a shared reserved-key guard (`if(key==='__proto__'||key==='constructor'||key==='prototype')continue;`) at each parsed-key assignment, or build maps with Object.create(null).

findings.rule_broken: Pass 4 string-built dynamic RegExp (ReDoS / regex injection)
findings.evidence: src/namespace.ts:28-30 matchGlob: builds `pat` by replacing only `.` and `*`, then `new RegExp('(^|/)'+pat+'($|/)')`. Other metachars ([ ( + ? \) pass through unescaped.
findings.impact: A namespaceGlobs entry with `[` or `(` yields invalid pattern → new RegExp throws SyntaxError crashing resolveNamespace (DoS); wildcard expansions can backtrack quadratically (ReDoS). Operator config, so low.
findings.correction: Escape all regex metacharacters in literal glob segments before substituting wildcard tokens (reuse comment.ts esc()), and/or wrap new RegExp in try/catch treating malformed glob as non-matching.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 7 transport/network alignment (no TLS/scheme enforcement; cleartext credential)
findings.evidence: src/llm.ts:26-31 `fetch(`${opts.baseUrl}/chat/completions`, {headers:{Authorization:`Bearer ${opts.apiKey}`}...})`. baseUrl is an arbitrary config string with no https:/scheme validation before the bearer token is attached.
findings.impact: If baseUrl is http:// or attacker-influenced, the API key is sent in cleartext / exfiltrated to an arbitrary egress target — contrary to the module's 'Local default makes NO external calls' comment. Operator-configured, low.
findings.correction: Reject non-https: baseUrl (or require explicit allow-insecure opt-in) before sending the Authorization header; optionally allowlist hosts.

findings.evidence: package.json:50-56 devDependencies all caret-ranged (typescript ^5.4.5, @types/node ^20.12.12, @types/jest ^29.5.12, jest ^29.7.0, ts-jest ^29.1.4). Mitigation: committed package-lock.json (v3) makes npm ci reproducible.
findings.impact: Floating ranges permit silent toolchain drift on fresh npm install (irreproducible builds), though lockfile pins npm ci. Dev-only; 0 runtime deps; npm audit reports 0 vulnerabilities; LICENSE MIT matches package.json.
findings.correction: Pin devDependencies to exact versions, or codify npm ci in CI and document the lockfile as pin authority.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 2 coverage-vs-criticality: critical path with zero real assertion coverage
findings.evidence: src/compiler.ts has zero test references (grep of tests/ for 'compiler' returns only tests/schema.test.ts:43 unrelated literal). Yet pipeline.ts:11 imports buildDedupReport et al and pipeline.ts:93 calls buildDedupReport every run.
findings.impact: Dedup detection and prompt/primitive index construction could silently return wrong groupings/twins/markdown with no failing test.
findings.correction: Add tests/compiler.test.ts asserting exact-twin grouping, uniqueCount arithmetic, isPromptMeta filtering, injectable filtering, and dedupReportToMarkdown shape.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 5 error-path & Pass 3 assertion-strength: external-IO code has happy-path-only tautological coverage
findings.evidence: tests/llm.test.ts:17-20 only asserts `expect(typeof a.classify).toBe('function')`. The network logic in src/llm.ts:22-38 (fetch POST, !res.ok branch, catch swallow, AbortController timeout) runs zero assertions.
findings.impact: A regression in request shape, auth header, non-2xx handling, JSON parsing, or timeout/abort ships undetected.
findings.correction: Test makeOpenAIAdapter with mocked fetch: assert URL/headers/body, non-ok→null, thrown/aborted→null, timer cleared, content returned for well-formed payload.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 2 coverage-vs-criticality: central transform tested only transitively with weak assertion
findings.evidence: src/normalize_meta.ts (buildMeta, serializeToYamlFrontMatter) has zero direct test imports. buildMeta (normalize_meta.ts:37-69) only exercised transitively via tests/pipeline_integration.test.ts:60 asserting `result.injected.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)`.
findings.impact: Wrong taxonomy mapping, sharing_scope/namespace resolution, or mis-serialized YAML scalar would not fail any test.
findings.correction: Add unit tests for buildMeta per artifact_type asserting emitted field set + taxonomy, plus serializeToYamlFrontMatter scalar quoting/escaping and empty-array handling.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 5 error-path & Pass 2: file-mutating module lacks dedicated unit tests for its branches
findings.evidence: src/inject.ts writes files (fs.writeFileSync at inject.ts:117,120,123) but no test imports ../src/inject; only transitive via pipeline_integration.test.ts. Sidecar branch (102-108), dry-run diff (112-118), sync injectFile (157-164), body-preservation path (135) have no direct assertions.
findings.impact: A regression in strategy resolution, dry-run vs write, or re-run idempotency could corrupt/duplicate content in real files with no failing test.
findings.correction: Add tests/inject.test.ts covering each strategy, dryRun (no mutation, diff written), re-run idempotency, and body-preservation (postInjectionBodyHash===originalBodyHash).

findings.rule_broken: Pass 7 type-safety: double-cast escape hatches defeat strict structural typing
findings.evidence: 7 `as unknown as` double-casts: inventory.ts:340 `return fields as unknown as NormalizedMeta`, inventory.ts:374,269,276, pipeline.ts:66,73, verify.ts:72. Each launders an arbitrary Record into NormalizedMeta with no runtime validation.
findings.impact: A NormalizedMeta shape drift compiles clean and surfaces only at runtime as malformed headers.
findings.correction: Replace double-casts with a validated narrowing (runtime shape guard) at the meta-bag boundary, or a single typed adapter with unit tests.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 2 stub detection: advertised feature is a placeholder returning a hardcoded empty value
findings.evidence: src/compiler.ts:17 buildDedupReport(entries,_threshold,_prefixLen) never uses the params and line 25 hardcodes `nearDuplicates: []`. Threshold is a real knob: schema.ts:155 nearDupThreshold, threaded through pipeline.ts:93.
findings.impact: Near-duplicate detection is silently a no-op; users configuring the threshold get false assurance while near-duplicate artifacts pass undetected.
findings.correction: Implement near-duplicate detection using hashPrefix/threshold and populate nearDuplicates, or remove nearDupThreshold from the config surface and document exact-twin-only.

findings.rule_broken: Pass 4 fake-validation: metadata-write failures are swallowed and reported as success
findings.evidence: src/inventory.ts:330 writeSidecar `catch { /* unwritable — recorded in manifest only */ }` and inventory.ts:349 writeFolderSidecar same; inventory.ts:304 swallows injectFile failure. Run returns success and manifest lists the record regardless of whether the sidecar was written.
findings.impact: On an unwritable target, metadata is never persisted yet the run reports success and the manifest implies the sidecar exists (fail-open).
findings.correction: Capture the write failure into the record/unknowns (push sidecar_write_failed:<path>:<err>) and surface a non-silent status; don't treat an unwritable target as success.

findings.evidence: src/pipeline.ts:61 `const cls = classify(e.sourcePath, body, e.headerConvention)` uses coarse classify(). src/classify.ts:82-88 classifyWithSemantics() adds .semantic via classifyArtifact(); grep shows it referenced only in tests/artifact_class.test.ts:7,68. normalize_meta.ts:37 buildMeta receives only ClassifyResult.
findings.impact: The fully implemented, tested 17-class semantic classifier never influences injected metadata; the classifyArtifact→pipeline edge is unconnected, so richer classification/placement is unreachable in production.
findings.correction: Wire classifyWithSemantics into pipeline.ts and thread ArtifactClassification into buildMeta (or MetaV3 taxonomy/placement planes), or remove the semantic layer if out of scope.

findings.evidence: src/placement_policy.ts:77 compilePlacementPlan and :128 compilePlacementPlans are exported; grep finds no runtime caller — only tests/placement_policy.test.ts. pipeline.ts imports (4-16) do not include placement_policy.
findings.impact: The placement compiler (target dir/layer, quarantine routing) is unreachable from the pipeline and runPipelineAsync; the capability to decide where artifacts live is never exercised outside unit tests.
findings.correction: Invoke compilePlacementPlans in the pipeline to populate placement metadata or emit a placement report, expose via CLI, or document as experimental/library-only.

findings.evidence: src/schema.ts:166-290 defines the MetaV3 nine-plane model (META_V3_SCHEMA_VERSION, META_V3_PLANES, IdentityPlane…LineagePlane, MetaV3). grep shows MetaV3/META_V3_* only in tests/schema.test.ts. No function converts NormalizedMeta→MetaV3.
findings.impact: The entire v3 metadata model is dead wiring: no producer emits it, no consumer reads it; the 'single plane' design goal is unrealized. Types are carried/versioned/tested but yield zero runtime capability.
findings.correction: Add a NormalizedMeta→MetaV3 builder wired into inject/index emission (≥1 live producer+consumer), or drop the v3 types until a consumer exists.

findings.evidence: src/assist.ts:54 `export async function isMateriallyBetter(...)` has zero callers (grep src/ + tests/). It duplicates reconcile_fields.ts:70 isMateriallyBetterLlm. Its only config gate AssistConfig.materialityCheck (assist.ts:10,16) is read solely at assist.ts:57 inside this dead function.
findings.impact: Exported dead function ships in the public API; the materialityCheck config knob has no live effect anywhere (orphaned config), misleading integrators.
findings.correction: Remove isMateriallyBetter (and materialityCheck if unused elsewhere), or route reconcile_fields through it to consolidate materiality logic.

findings.evidence: src/namespace.ts:21 optional namespaceGlobs is read at namespace.ts:67-69, but pipeline.ts:19 toCfg builds NamespaceConfig without it, PipelineConfig (schema.ts:148-164) has no namespaceGlobs field, and grep shows it set only in tests/namespace.test.ts:13.
findings.impact: Per-glob namespace override is implemented and read but cannot be configured through the pipeline/public run path; reachable only by low-level library callers, so the feature is effectively off in production.
findings.correction: Add namespaceGlobs to PipelineConfig and pass through toCfg, or document the override as available only via direct resolveNamespace use.

findings.evidence: src/namespace.ts:20 declares required promptGlob:string; pipeline.ts:19 sets promptGlob:'Prompt-*.md' (hardcoded). grep shows namespace.ts never reads cfg.promptGlob — resolveNamespace reads only cfg.namespace and cfg.namespaceGlobs.
findings.impact: A required config field is manufactured on every call but never consumed, implying prompt-glob configurability that doesn't exist and forcing all callers/tests to supply a dead value.
findings.correction: Remove promptGlob from NamespaceConfig (and the hardcoded value in toCfg), or consume it where prompt globbing is decided.

findings.evidence: src/reconcile_fields.ts:36 LLM_MATERIALITY_FIELDS=Set(['description','intent']) and assist.ts:32 PROSE_ORIGIN_FIELDS includes 'intent'. But no meta type in schema.ts declares an intent field and buildMeta (37-69) never emits one; grep 'intent' in src shows only set-membership refs.
findings.impact: The 'intent' prose/materiality handling is unreachable via generated metadata; only pre-existing hand-authored intent: keys could trigger it — a near-dead branch suggesting an unsupported field.
findings.correction: Add intent to the metadata schema and have buildMeta populate it, or drop 'intent' from LLM_MATERIALITY_FIELDS and PROSE_ORIGIN_FIELDS.

findings.rule_broken: dormant_capability (reusable primitive not on public API)
findings.evidence: src/index.ts:1-20 re-exports 18 modules but neither ./comment nor ./compiler. Both imported only internally — comment.ts by inject/verify/pipeline/retrieval/classify/inventory; compiler.ts by pipeline.ts:11.
findings.impact: Two reusable primitives are hidden from consumers: the filetype injection-strategy engine (comment.ts) and the dedup/library-index compiler (compiler.ts). Consumers can't reuse strategy resolution or dedup reporting.
findings.correction: If reuse is intended, re-export selected comment.ts/compiler.ts symbols from index.ts; if internal-by-design, document they are deliberately private.

findings.evidence: src/schema.ts:117-123 FieldDiff (action union inline) and src/reconcile_fields.ts:14-22 FieldDiff (action:ReconcileAction) are two structurally identical definitions. inject.ts:13 imports FieldDiff from ./schema yet writeInjection (inject.ts:110) receives diffs from reconcileFields. index.ts:6-7 exports both (FieldDiff and ReconcileFieldDiff).
findings.impact: Two sources of truth for one diff contract; type-check passes only by structural coincidence. Changing an action value on one side silently diverges the other; public API exposes two names for one concept.
findings.correction: Delete reconcile_fields.FieldDiff; import canonical FieldDiff + exported action type from schema.ts in reconcile_fields.ts and inject.ts; drop the duplicate public export.

findings.evidence: src/schema.ts:14 and src/namespace.ts:4 both declare SharingScope='private'|'shared'|'agnostic'. resolveNamespace returns namespace.SharingScope; normalize_meta.ts:48 re-casts `sharing_scope: sharingScope as SharingScope` (schema's type).
findings.impact: SharingScope defined twice; a new value added to one won't propagate, and the explicit cast at the boundary masks divergence at compile time.
findings.correction: Define SharingScope once in schema.ts, import into namespace.ts; remove the `as SharingScope` cast so the type flows structurally.

findings.evidence:
Identical LLM materiality prompt duplicated verbatim at src/assist.ts:62 and src/reconcile_fields.ts:75 (`Field: ...
A:...
B:...
Is B materially more informative than A? Reply only: yes or no`), both parsed via `.trim().toLowerCase().startsWith('yes')`.
findings.impact: Prompt wording and yes/no parsing contract live in two places; changing phrasing/format in one silently drifts the other, so identical inputs could yield different reconcile decisions by path.
findings.correction: Extract a single materiality-judgment primitive (prompt builder + response parser) called from both assist.ts and reconcile_fields.ts.

findings.evidence: src/namespace.ts:13-22 NamespaceConfig requires authority, nearDupThreshold, hashPrefixLength, outputDir, indexDir, promptGlob (+namespace, namespaceGlobs?). Its only consumer resolveNamespace reads only cfg.namespace and cfg.namespaceGlobs. pipeline.ts:18-20 toCfg manufactures all six unused fields from PipelineConfig.
findings.impact: Consumer's required interface far wider than used; every caller/test supplies six irrelevant fields, and the duplicated set couples NamespaceConfig to PipelineConfig with no compiler-enforced link.
findings.correction: Narrow NamespaceConfig to what resolveNamespace reads ({namespace; namespaceGlobs?}), or derive from PipelineConfig via Pick, eliminating the field duplication in toCfg.

findings.evidence: inject.ts:147,159 pass Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(meta)) (typed NormalizedMeta) into reconcileFields, whose signature is Record<string,unknown> (reconcile_fields.ts:81-83,137-139). existingMeta comes from parseExistingMeta (inject.ts:20-42), which heuristically re-types YAML (ints only via /^\d+$/ at inject.ts:36, else string/list).
findings.impact: NormalizedMeta is erased to Record<string,unknown> at the reconcile boundary; typed producer and heuristic parser can disagree on runtime type (e.g. float token_cost_estimate parses to string), and isMateriallyBetterSync compares JSON.stringify lengths across mismatched types, mis-deciding keep/revise.
findings.correction: Type the reconcile edge on a shared metadata record type and normalize parseExistingMeta output to the exact field types buildMeta emits (numbers, booleans, arrays).

findings.evidence: src/llm.ts:33-37 — `if (!res.ok) return null;` then `} catch { return null; }` in makeOpenAIAdapter.classify; abort/timeout, non-2xx status+body, and JSON-parse failures all collapsed into bare `null` with zero logging.
findings.impact: Every LLM failure mode (timeout, 401/429/5xx, malformed body) is indistinguishable from a legitimate 'no' answer; silent degradation to heuristic values is undiagnosable.
findings.correction: Emit structured diagnostics on failure branches (log res.status + truncated body; log err.name/message distinguishing AbortError/timeout from network/parse) and surface per-call outcome (ok|http_error|timeout|parse_error).

findings.evidence: src/pipeline.ts:91-103 — verify(...) produces VerifyResult[] (incl. bodyPreserved=false) but pipeline never inspects `issues`; only writes verification-report.json gated behind `if (!config.dryRun)`; returns success regardless.
findings.impact: verify.ts:60 flags body-hash-mismatch corruption but that signal reaches only a JSON file nothing reads. A run that corrupted file bodies is reported as clean success.
findings.correction: Consume verify signal: aggregate issues, log a summary, and throw or return `hadVerificationFailures` so CI can gate. Don't gate the in-memory result behind dryRun.

findings.evidence: src/pipeline.ts:49 `if (spec.strategy === 'skip-binary') continue;` and src/pipeline.ts:83 `if (!meta || !meta.injectable) continue;` skip files with no record; runPipelineAsync emits no run summary.
findings.impact: Library path produces zero operator-visible coverage signal (scanned vs injected vs skipped vs verify-failed); silent coverage holes.
findings.correction: Return/log a coverage tally (scanned, injected, skippedBinary, skippedNonInjectable, verifyFailed) with skipped source paths.

findings.evidence: src/inventory.ts:302-304 — `try { injectFile(...) } catch { if (targetIncludes(schema,'sidecar')) writeSidecar(...) }` discards the injection error; if schema doesn't target sidecar, no fallback AND no record.
findings.impact: A file whose header injection throws is silently downgraded or produces nothing, with no entry in unknowns and no log.
findings.correction: Push `header_injection_failed:${msg}` onto rec.unknowns (mirroring inventory.ts:204-206) and log before falling back to sidecar.

findings.evidence: src/inventory.ts:318-326 safeRead: `} catch { return null; }` — read/open error returns same `null` used to signal 'binary file'; caller inventory.ts:294 sets strategy='skip-binary' on null.
findings.impact: Permission-denied/IO error on a text file is silently reclassified as 'binary skipped'; coverage gaps from access errors are invisible.
findings.correction: Differentiate the catch from the binary sentinel (record `read_failed:${msg}`/log) rather than returning the same null.

findings.evidence: src/inventory.ts:330 `catch { /* unwritable — recorded in manifest only */ }` and inventory.ts:349 `catch { /* unwritable dir — recorded, skip */ }` — comments claim recorded but neither pushes to unknowns nor logs.
findings.impact: Sidecar/folder-sidecar write failures are invisible; metadata silently not persisted, contradicting the reassuring comment.
findings.correction: Record `sidecar_write_failed:${msg}` on unknowns and/or warn; a no-op catch on a write is a swallowed failure.

findings.evidence: src/inventory.ts:167-169 walk(): `try { entries = fs.readdirSync(...) } catch { return; }` aborts recursion into the entire subtree with no record/log.
findings.impact: An unreadable directory silently drops its whole subtree; manifest totals look complete but under-count.
findings.correction: On readdir catch, emit a diagnostic and record skipped dir (synthetic record or top-level skippedDirs list).

findings.evidence: src/retrieval.ts:18-31 looksBinaryOnDisk(): `} catch { return true; }`, consumed at retrieval.ts:57 `if (!knownText && looksBinaryOnDisk(full)) continue;` — unopenable file treated as binary and excluded.
findings.impact: Unknown-extension text file that errors on open is silently dropped from the whole pipeline; missing outputs untraceable to read failure.
findings.correction: Log open/read errors distinctly from the binary decision; surface excluded-due-to-error paths.

findings.evidence: src/reconcile_fields.ts:74/77 and src/assist.ts:48-50 fall back to heuristic/seed when adapter.classify absent or returns null; recorded FieldDiff reason (reconcile_fields.ts:126-127) can read 'content-size heuristic' even when an LLM path failed.
findings.impact: No signal distinguishes 'LLM said no' from 'LLM failed/returned null and we fell back'; degraded runs indistinguishable from healthy ones in the audit trail.
findings.correction: Record actual path taken (llm_ok|llm_failed_fallback|no_adapter|heuristic) in FieldDiff reason and a run-level counter.

findings.evidence: src/llm.ts:17-40 makeOpenAIAdapter emits no call count/latency/token/failure metric; src/pipeline.ts loops assistField (69) and injectFileAsync (86) with no timing/accounting.
findings.impact: No observable signal for LLM call volume, cost, latency, or failure rate; cost/latency regressions undiagnosable.
findings.correction: Instrument adapter/pipeline with counters/timings (calls, failures, tokens, p50/p95) exported to a sink.
